title: Buttondown vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is right for your business.
h1: Buttondown vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Buttondown and Constant Contact serve different segments of the email marketing market. Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers seeking a markdown-first, lightweight platform, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and local businesses needing comprehensive marketing automation. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right tool for your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You want to start for free with a fully functional email marketing platform
You prefer writing newsletters in markdown or have API integration needs
You're a developer, technical writer, or indie creator managing a small subscriber base
choose_b_if: You need advanced features like SMS marketing, event management, and e-commerce integration
You're running a nonprofit or local business requiring comprehensive marketing tools
You want built-in social media advertising and list-building capabilities

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. Buttondown excels for technical users and offers better value with its free tier, earning higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.8 vs 4.0 on G2). Constant Contact is better if you need advanced marketing features like SMS, event management, and e-commerce tools that go beyond basic email marketing.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, starting completely free for up to 100 subscribers with a $9/month paid plan. Constant Contact has no free tier and starts at $12/month. For small publishers and newsletters, Buttondown offers better pricing.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import and export functionality. Buttondown specifically offers import/export capabilities, making it relatively straightforward to migrate your subscriber list. However, you'll lose access to Constant Contact's advanced features like SMS and event management if you switch.
